title: run_on_new_thread scoped-thread pattern is the correct fix for current_thread runtimes
description: block_in_place and Handle::block_on both panic when called from within a current_thread runtime. The scoped-thread approach (spawn a new OS thread with its own runtime) is the only safe option. The scope() guarantees the result is always set before the expect(). Future contributors might be tempted to use block_in_place — this design comment must be preserved.
